LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO (AMENDED) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE COURT MAY NOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL SEPARATE STATEMENTS; THE COURT MAY ONLY CONSIDER THE SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1350(B). THE SEPARATE STATEMENT IS DEFECTIVE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT IDENTIFY EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, ISSUE OR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE AND EACH PIECE OF EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT THEREOF. NEITHER THE CAUSES OF ACTION NOT THE ELEMENTS ARE IDENTIFIED. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ADDRESS DAMAGES. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARL SAUCEDA, CSR 2031

Notice Of Mot And Mot To Enforce Settlement And Set Aside Dismissal (Cont From March 19, 2010 Hearing)
MINI MINUTES: Dept 3, YGC, Judge Tang; Clerk: Mary Shea; CSR: Gordon Aiavao #11216
Plaintiff Virginia Baldovieso represented by: Sydney Hall - present
Defendnat Catherine Branham represented by: Jonathan Scott Weaver - present

Motion to enforce settlement is granted, set aside of dismissal not necessary.

Judgment is as follows:
Writ of possession of 179 Tunnell Avenue San Francisco for Plaintiff Baldovieso and against
Defendant Theodore and Catherine Branham.

Writ of execution for possession of 179 Tunnel Ave not to be executed upon until after 5-15-10.

Plaintiff agrees to waive all damages up to this point, except for waste, on condition that defendant and all other residents move out of the premises on or before 5-15-10.

Sheriff may post "Notice of Possession" before 5-15-10.

(Amended) Notice Of Hearing Of Pltf.'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO (AMENDED) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: The motion is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of proof. The Court may not consider plaintiff's supplemental separate statements; the Court may only consider the separate statement filed with the moving papers. Plaintiff failed to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1350(b). The separate statement is defective because it does not identify each cause of action, issue or affirmative defense and each piece of evidence in support thereof. Neither the causes of action not the elements are identified. Finally, plaintiff failed to address damages. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CARL SAUCEDA, CSR 2031

(Amended) Notice Of Hearing Of Pltf.'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO MAY 16, 2008 PER AMENDED NOTICE. (302/CK) JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW, CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 9689

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Compel Dfs To Further Respond To Discovery & For Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Sydney J. Hall, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Virginia Baldovieso
Jonathan S. Weaver, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Catherine Branham

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Virginia Baldovieso's motion to compel further responses to request for production and form interrogatories (set #1) is granted in part and denied in part.

Form interrogatories
16.1 - 16.7, 16.9, 16.10
Denied.

50.1 - 50.6
Granted. Defendants may define any agreements and shall answer those interrogatories that may apply

Request for production of documents
5 -11
Granted. Defendant shall comply with CCP 2031.210 and 2031.230.

Where the Court has ordered a further response, defendant shall comply within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2008 Set For Trial On Aug-18-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-30-08 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 504
